name: Plandex
pricing_notes: Open source self-hosted free; Plandex Cloud: free tier, Pro $10/mo; pay your own LLM API costs

auth.notes: Self-hosted: no auth; Cloud: account token + LLM provider API keys configured

performance.notes: Task duration depends entirely on LLM calls; multi-file changes streamed progressively

agent_readiness.notes: Terminal-based AI coding agent focused on large multi-file tasks. Git-integrated version control of AI changes. Sandboxed plan execution with review before apply. Supports long-horizon tasks across many files. CLI primary interface. Self-hostable. Works with any OpenAI-compatible LLM. Diff-based change application with conflict resolution.

gotchas: CLI-only — no IDE plugin or web UI (terminal-first tool)
Self-hosted setup requires managing server infrastructure
Less active development than Cursor or Aider
Long-horizon task quality depends heavily on LLM choice
best_when: Terminal-centric developers who prefer CLI over IDE plugins
Large multi-file refactoring tasks that need human review before apply
Self-hosted requirements where Cursor/Copilot cloud isn't acceptable
Teams wanting open-source alternative to Devin for multi-file coding
avoid_when: Developers preferring IDE-integrated AI (use Cursor or Continue.dev)
Rapid single-file iteration (Aider or direct LLM chat faster)
Teams needing active community and frequent releases
summary: Plandex is a terminal-based AI coding agent specialized for large multi-file tasks with git-integrated version control of changes. Plans are reviewed before application. Self-hostable open source with cloud option. Works with any OpenAI-compatible LLM. Best for terminal-centric developers tackling large refactoring or feature implementation across many files who want to review AI changes before committing.
